title: A Tesla on Autopilot Crashed Into Her House. She Didn't Make It.
script: Tesla drove into her house! On Friday evening in Katy, Texas, a Tesla Model 3 left the road and crashed through the side of a residential home. Inside was Martha Avila, 76 years old. She died from her injuries. The driver, Michael Butler, said he was using the car's Autopilot feature at the time — that the car was driving itself when it drifted out of its lane. A Ring doorbell camera recorded the moment of impact. Butler showed no signs of intoxication and is cooperating with investigators. The crash is now tied to a 3.2-million-vehicle federal probe by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, already at Engineering Analysis — the final step before a recall demand. A grandmother is dead. A 3.2-million-car recall is on the table. Was Autopilot to blame for Martha Avila's death?
